task: Explain why the proposed debugging trajectory dataset schema feels reasonable while clarifying what makes it strong and what still needs tightening.
step: explain why proposed schema feels reasonable
summary: Explained that the proposed schema feels reasonable because it captures the right causal unit for agent learning: problem/context, observed steps, knowledge used, solution, and verified outcome. Clarified that the concept is strong but should be tightened with reproducibility, provenance, verification, and Datalox skill/note linkage before being called production-grade.
observations: The schema is reasonable because it is outcome-labeled and follows problem -> context -> action trajectory -> knowledge -> solution -> outcome.
It matches Datalox's own trace-to-note-to-skill mental model, even though it currently presents the trace as the product instead of an export of a knowledge system.
The missing production fields are mostly around trust and reuse: schema version, repro commands, environment snapshot, evidence references, provenance/license/redaction, and verification artifacts.
title: Explained that the proposed schema feels reasonable because it captures the right causal unit for agent learning: probl…
signal: User asked why the original proposed schema feels very reasonable.
interpretation: The user's intuition is sound because the schema captures the learning unit that agent/eval buyers care about; the main issue is not conceptual correctness but export rigor.
recommendedAction: Keep the proposed shape as the conceptual v1, then harden it with reproducibility and evidence fields rather than replacing it wholesale.
